beats.narration: Calibration blocks are flat — most welds live on pipe. The moment the surface curves, your coupling, beam, and plotting all change.

payload.title: CURVED SURFACES
payload.sub: What happens when the entry surface curves.

beats.narration: A flat wedge touches a pipe on one line: weak coupling, a rocking probe, a wandering exit point. Gap over half a millimeter? Contour it.

payload.heading: Line contact: contour the shoe
payload.note: GAP OVER 0.5 MM (0.020 IN)? CONTOUR IT

beats.narration: Around the pipe, the beam is a chord. If sine theta exceeds I D over O D, it never reaches the bore.

payload.heading: Can the beam even reach the bore?
payload.note: SIN θ ≤ ID ÷ OD — OR IT NEVER REACHES THE BORE

beats.narration: Run the list: fit the contour to the scan, re-verify exit point and angle, check the reach, calibrate on matching curvature.

chips.t: Fit the contour to the scan
Check reach: sin θ ≤ ID ÷ OD

beats.narration: So — fit it, reach it, match it: contour the shoe, prove the beam reaches the bore, and calibrate on the curvature you scan.

payload.sub: Calibrate on the curvature you actually scan.
